key_insight: Structural uncertainty measures how consistently an LLM can rank its own reasoning paths, revealing reliability signals beyond simple answer disagreement.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to flag brittle reasoning in math and logic systems, especially when multiple candidate solutions are available for self-consistency checks.
method: Generate multiple candidate solutions for each query.
Use the model to make pairwise self-preference judgments among its own outputs.
Aggregate preferences with Bradley-Terry + PageRank, then split uncertainty into across-trial instability and within-trial ambiguity.
evidence_signal: Across five LLMs and eight benchmarks, the structural signal complements answer dispersion and improves unreliable-instance detection on logical and mathematical tasks.
watch_item: The method becomes uninformative in factual retrieval settings, where structural uncertainty collapses toward uniformity and may not distinguish confidence well.
paper_title: Quantifying Consistency in LLM Logical Reasoning via Structural Uncertainty
